mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS remains in the lower regime.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1% do not exceed baseline by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window following trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series decline simultaneously, as it indicates broad-based consumption and investment weakness.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 479k units suggests moderate housing activity
failure_condition: Joint decline trigger produces negative 20-day returns in < 55% of instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below the prior 3-month average signals supply-side weakness and reduced productive capacity utilization. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as it indicates deteriorating corporate profitability and economic slack.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following trigger are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate signals robust labor market momentum. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both conditions hold, indicating strong consumer income growth and spending capacity.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k month-over-month AND UNRATE stable or declining
regime_hint: labor market expansion or late-cycle employment strength
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above 0.5% signals accelerating inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates, compressing equity valuations. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as inflation expectations rise and real discount rates increase.
regime_hint: inflationary pressure or monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) below the prior 2-quarter average signals economic contraction. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when GDP growth turns negative, as it indicates recession onset and earnings deterioration.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during negative GDP growth quarters are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 500,000 units combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand and construction activity. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both demand indicators strengthen simultaneously, indicating robust economic momentum and corporate earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 500k units AND RSAFS increases > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 479k units, RSAFS at 389.3B dollars
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
